facts: NIT Sikkim (National Institute of Technology Sikkim) is located at Ravangla, Barfung Road, Namchi District, Sikkim 737139.
Languages spoken: Mostly Hindi. Local people speak Nepali, Bengali, and Hindi.
Climate: Mostly winter weather. Non-stop rain during rainy season. Summer mornings are sunny but afternoon rain is likely.
New Jalpaiguri Station is 120 km from NIT Sikkim.
Bagdogra Airport is 130 km from NIT Sikkim.
From airport/station: Take SNT bus or share taxi available till 1 PM. Share taxi costs Rs 450, or Rs 800-900 if there is a landslide on the road.
Separate cab from airport/station to NIT Sikkim costs Rs 4,000.
Daily bus available at 1 PM, fare Rs 280.
BH-1 (Boys Hostel 1) for 2nd year and above, located in campus.
BH-2 (Boys Hostel 2) for 2nd year and above, located in campus.
1st year boys hostel is located 1.5 km outside the main campus.
MTech students have 2 hostels within campus.
PhD students do not have hostel facility.
Faculty and staff have a separate FS hostel on campus.
NIT Sikkim has 3 girls hostels.
GH-1 (Girls Hostel 1), located in campus.
GH-2 (Girls Hostel 2), located in campus.
GH-3 (Girls Hostel 3), located in campus.

mess.structure: 1 boys mess (serves all boys including MTech and PhD) and 1 girls mess (serves all girls). Same menu for everyone.

friday.dinner: Paneer Chilli/Chicken Chilli OR Biryani (alternate weeks)

mess.notes: Biryani served every 2 weeks (alternate Fridays). Menu covers South Indian, North Indian, and Sikkim food.
mess.cuisine: Mix of North Indian staples (parathas, dal, rajma, chole bhatura), South Indian items (dosa, idli, uttapam, medu vada), and non-veg options (butter chicken, egg curry, fish curry, chicken chilli, biryani). Sikkim food also included.

fee_structure.notes: Mess charges are separate from hostel and tuition fees.

facts: 10 GB per day per student at NIT Sikkim.
Uninterrupted WiFi connectivity in hostel rooms, classrooms, and labs.
75% attendance is mandatory at NIT Sikkim.
Remaining 25% can be covered by medical certificates.
Udgam: Cultural fest.
Abhiyantran: Technical fest.
2 fests held per year.
1-2 washing machines available per hostel.
Costs Rs 50 per wash.
No student elections are held at NIT Sikkim.
Cricket, Football, Volleyball, Badminton, Kho-Kho, Basketball, Chess, and Carrom are available.
Annual sports event involving the entire college including faculty.
On-campus canteen serving snacks and food.
Small canteen located 100 meters outside campus.
Grocery shop 1 km away from campus.
2 restaurants 2 km away from campus.
Market is 2 km away from NIT Sikkim campus.
Misbehavior: Rs 20,000 fine plus deregistration for 1 semester. Very strictly enforced.
Property damage: Rs 20,000 to Rs 50,000 fine. Very strictly enforced.
Boys only gym at NIT Sikkim.
Free for students.
Accessible at any time.
Health centre open 8 AM to 10 PM.
Ambulance available for emergencies.
College bears testing expenses.
Students must buy medicines themselves if not available at the health centre.

nearby: Many restaurants in the market 2 km away from campus

malls: No malls nearby. Zudio, Trends, Reliance etc. are 26 km away.

facts: Mostly Hindi spoken at NIT Sikkim. Local people speak Nepali, Bengali, and Hindi.
Mostly winter weather at NIT Sikkim (Ravangla). Non-stop rain during rainy season. Summer mornings sunny but afternoon rain is likely.
NIT Sikkim offers B.Tech in CSE (Computer Science), ECE (Electronics & Communication), and EE (Electrical Engineering).
Timetable covers ECE 4th, 6th, and 8th semester; CSE 4th and 6th semester; EE 4th semester.
Subjects include Signal Processing, Analog Electronics, Microprocessor, Communication Systems, VLSI, Embedded Systems, and more.
